designations.designation: PHI Emotional Distress - Depression symptom score
In the past 7 days, self-reported depression severity rating

definitions.definition: Total score on a standardized depression screening questionnaire. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Emotional Distress - Anxiety symptom score
Over the past 2 weeks, self-reported generalized anxiety severity level

definitions.definition: Total score on a validated generalized anxiety screening instrument. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Emotional Distress - Perceived stress level
During the past 4 weeks, rating of overall perceived psychological stress

definitions.definition: Self-rated global stress intensity over the assessment time period. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Emotional Distress - Emotional regulation score
In the past 7 days, ability to manage and modulate emotional responses

definitions.definition: Rating of capacity to regulate emotional reactions under stress. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Emotional Distress - Suicidal ideation screen result
Over the past 2 weeks, presence of thoughts of self-harm or suicide

definitions.definition: Binary screening result for active or passive suicidal ideation. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Emotional Distress - Overall psychiatric symptom severity
During the past 4 weeks, global rating of psychiatric symptom burden

definitions.definition: Composite severity score across all psychiatric symptom domains. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: COAS Psychological Well-Being - Social functioning score
In the past 7 days, ability to engage in social interactions and relationships

definitions.definition: Rating of social engagement and interpersonal functioning level. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Psychological Well-Being - Occupational functioning rating
Over the past 2 weeks, impact of mental health on work or school performance

definitions.definition: Degree of mental-health-related impairment in occupational roles. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Psychological Well-Being - Psychological resilience score
During the past 4 weeks, capacity to recover from adverse psychological experiences

definitions.definition: Rating of adaptive coping ability following stressful life events. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Psychological Well-Being - Self-efficacy assessment
In the past 7 days, belief in ability to accomplish daily tasks

definitions.definition: Self-rated confidence in managing routine activities and challenges. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Psychological Well-Being - Mental health functional impairment
Over the past 2 weeks, degree of disability caused by mental health symptoms

definitions.definition: Overall functional limitation attributable to psychiatric symptoms. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Psychological Well-Being - Trauma exposure history
During the past 4 weeks, prior exposure to potentially traumatic events

definitions.definition: Count of distinct traumatic events endorsed on screening checklist. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: Psychotropic medication usage
Current use of prescribed psychiatric medications

definitions.definition: Record of prescribed psychotropic medications and current dosages. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Psychological distress thermometer
Visual analog rating of overall psychological distress

definitions.definition: Single-item distress rating on a zero-to-ten visual analog scale. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Substance use screening result
Screening for problematic alcohol or drug use

definitions.definition: Outcome of a brief standardized substance use screening instrument. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Age at mental health assessment
In the past 7 days, patient age at the time of psychiatric evaluation

definitions.definition: Chronological age at the date of mental health screening visit, assessed in the past 7 days.

designations.designation: Psychiatric comorbidity count
Over the past 2 weeks, number of concurrent psychiatric diagnoses

definitions.definition: Total number of active comorbid psychiatric conditions diagnosed, assessed over the past 2 weeks.

designations.designation: Mental health quality of life rating
Impact of psychiatric symptoms on overall quality of life

definitions.definition: Self-reported effect of mental illness on daily well-being.

designations.designation: Psychiatric treatment response
Clinical response to current mental health treatment

definitions.definition: Clinician-rated response to prescribed psychiatric intervention.

designations.designation: Panic attack frequency
Number of panic episodes in the assessment period

definitions.definition: Count of discrete panic attacks during the assessment interval.

designations.designation: PHI Physical Limitation - Joint pain severity score
In the past 7 days, self-reported severity of joint pain at the most affected site

definitions.definition: A numeric rating of the patient's joint pain intensity on a validated ordinal scale. Joint pain severity is assessed at the most affected joint and guides analgesic therapy decisions. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Physical Limitation - Active range of motion
Over the past 2 weeks, angular excursion of the joint during voluntary movement

definitions.definition: The maximum angular displacement achieved through voluntary muscle contraction at the specified joint, measured in degrees with a goniometer. Reduced active range indicates pathology. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Physical Limitation - Grip strength measurement
During the past 4 weeks, maximum force exerted during a hand grip dynamometry test

definitions.definition: Peak isometric grip force measured in kilograms using a calibrated hand dynamometer. Grip strength is a surrogate marker of upper-extremity musculoskeletal function. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Physical Limitation - Muscle weakness grading
In the past 7 days, manual muscle testing grade for the affected muscle group

definitions.definition: A clinical grade assigned to the strength of a target muscle group using the Medical Research Council scale from zero to five. Grades below four indicate clinically meaningful weakness. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Physical Limitation - Bone mineral density T-score
Over the past 2 weeks, standard deviation of bone density relative to young adult reference

definitions.definition: The number of standard deviations by which the patient's bone mineral density differs from the mean of a healthy young adult reference population, measured by dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Physical Limitation - Musculoskeletal symptom severity
During the past 4 weeks, global rating of musculoskeletal symptom burden

definitions.definition: A composite score reflecting the overall severity of musculoskeletal symptoms including pain, stiffness, and functional limitation assessed on a validated outcome measure. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: COAS Rehabilitation Progress - Gait speed assessment
In the past 7 days, walking velocity measured over a standardized distance

definitions.definition: The patient's habitual walking speed in meters per second over a four-meter course. Gait speed is a reliable predictor of disability, falls, and mortality in older adults. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Rehabilitation Progress - Fall risk score
Over the past 2 weeks, composite assessment of the patient's risk of falling

definitions.definition: A multifactorial risk score incorporating balance, gait speed, lower-extremity strength, medication use, and prior fall history. Elevated scores guide fall prevention interventions. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Rehabilitation Progress - Swollen joint count
During the past 4 weeks, number of joints with detectable swelling on examination

definitions.definition: The count of joints exhibiting clinically detectable soft-tissue swelling on standardized musculoskeletal examination. A core disease activity measure in arthritis. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Rehabilitation Progress - Morning stiffness duration
In the past 7 days, length of time morning joint stiffness persists after waking

definitions.definition: The duration in minutes from waking to resolution of joint stiffness. Morning stiffness lasting more than thirty minutes is characteristic of inflammatory arthropathies. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Rehabilitation Progress - Musculoskeletal functional impairment
Over the past 2 weeks, degree of disability in daily activities due to musculoskeletal conditions

definitions.definition: A standardized rating of the patient's functional limitation attributable to musculoskeletal disorders, covering self-care, mobility, and household activities. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Rehabilitation Progress - Physical therapy response
During the past 4 weeks, clinical improvement following physical rehabilitation program

definitions.definition: The change in functional outcome measures after a structured physical therapy program, expressed as the percentage improvement from baseline values. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: Musculoskeletal medication usage
Current medications prescribed for musculoskeletal conditions

definitions.definition: A record of analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and disease-modifying agents prescribed for the patient's musculoskeletal condition. Medication type, dose, and frequency are documented. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Vertebral fracture assessment
Presence and grading of vertebral compression fractures

definitions.definition: An evaluation of thoracolumbar vertebral bodies for morphometric fractures using lateral spine imaging. Vertebral fractures indicate elevated future fracture risk. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Disability index score
Standardized disability rating for musculoskeletal conditions

definitions.definition: A patient-reported disability score derived from a validated questionnaire assessing difficulty with daily activities. The index quantifies functional impact across life domains. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Age at musculoskeletal assessment
In the past 7 days, patient age at the time of musculoskeletal evaluation

definitions.definition: The chronological age of the patient at the date of the musculoskeletal assessment. Age is a major determinant of osteoarthritis prevalence and normative strength values, assessed in the past 7 days.

designations.designation: Musculoskeletal comorbidity count
Over the past 2 weeks, number of concurrent conditions affecting the musculoskeletal system

definitions.definition: The total number of active comorbid conditions relevant to musculoskeletal health including osteoporosis, fibromyalgia, gout, and rheumatic diseases, assessed over the past 2 weeks.

designations.designation: Musculoskeletal quality of life
Impact of musculoskeletal conditions on overall quality of life

definitions.definition: A patient-reported measure of the effect of musculoskeletal disorders on physical, emotional, and social well-being beyond clinical severity measures.

designations.designation: Musculoskeletal treatment response
Clinical response to musculoskeletal treatment regimen

definitions.definition: The clinician's assessment of the patient's response to prescribed musculoskeletal treatment covering pain reduction, functional improvement, and side effect burden.

designations.designation: Tender joint count
Number of joints painful to palpation on physical examination

definitions.definition: The count of joints eliciting pain on standardized palpation during musculoskeletal examination. A core component of composite disease activity indices in arthritis.

designations.designation: PHI Digestive Function - Abdominal pain severity score
In the past 7 days, self-reported severity of abdominal pain over the assessment period

definitions.definition: The patient's numeric rating of abdominal pain intensity on a validated ordinal scale, assessed at each visit and averaged over the assessment period. Abdominal pain is the most common presenting symptom of gastrointestinal disease and its severity guides diagnostic urgency and analgesic intervention. Pain severity is interpreted alongside location, character, and temporal pattern to narrow the differential diagnosis. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Digestive Function - Stool frequency per day
Over the past 2 weeks, average number of bowel movements per day during the recall period

definitions.definition: The mean daily number of bowel movements recorded by the patient over the assessment recall period, typically one to four weeks. Stool frequency is a primary symptom measure in both constipation and diarrhea-predominant conditions. Frequencies below three per week suggest constipation, while frequencies above three per day with loose consistency suggest diarrhea. Change in stool frequency from baseline is a key clinical trial endpoint. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Digestive Function - Bristol stool form scale score
During the past 4 weeks, classification of stool consistency using the seven-type Bristol chart

definitions.definition: The patient's self-reported stool consistency classified according to the seven-type Bristol stool form scale, where type one represents hard separate lumps and type seven represents entirely liquid stool. Types three and four are considered normal. The Bristol scale provides a standardized, reproducible measure of colonic transit time and is widely used as an endpoint in clinical trials of gastrointestinal motility disorders. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Digestive Function - Nausea severity rating
In the past 7 days, self-reported intensity of nausea symptoms during the assessment period

definitions.definition: A numeric rating of nausea intensity on a standardized visual analog or ordinal scale, recorded at each assessment. Nausea is a nonspecific gastrointestinal symptom that occurs across a wide range of conditions including gastroparesis, functional dyspepsia, and chemotherapy-induced emesis. Severity assessment captures both the peak intensity and the average daily burden of nausea and guides antiemetic prescribing decisions. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Digestive Function - Gastroesophageal reflux symptom score
Over the past 2 weeks, composite score of heartburn and regurgitation severity and frequency

definitions.definition: A composite score derived from a validated questionnaire assessing the frequency and severity of gastroesophageal reflux symptoms including heartburn, acid regurgitation, and chest discomfort. Higher scores indicate more severe and frequent reflux episodes. The GERD symptom score is used to monitor response to proton pump inhibitor therapy, identify patients requiring further endoscopic evaluation, and classify reflux disease severity. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: PHI Digestive Function - Gastrointestinal symptom severity
During the past 4 weeks, global rating of gastrointestinal symptom burden across all domains

definitions.definition: A composite severity rating reflecting the overall burden of gastrointestinal symptoms experienced by the patient, encompassing abdominal pain, nausea, altered bowel habits, bloating, and dysphagia. The severity score is derived from a validated multi-domain gastrointestinal symptom questionnaire and provides a single summary measure of symptom impact. Global severity ratings complement individual symptom assessments by capturing cumulative burden on daily functioning. As part of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: COAS Somatic Symptom Burden - Dysphagia severity grade
In the past 7 days, grade of swallowing difficulty assessed by clinical evaluation

definitions.definition: A graded assessment of the patient's difficulty in swallowing solids, liquids, or both, classified according to a standardized severity scale. Dysphagia grading considers the consistency of foods that can be safely swallowed, the frequency of difficulty, and the presence of aspiration risk. Moderate to severe dysphagia may necessitate dietary modification, swallowing rehabilitation, or endoscopic intervention. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Somatic Symptom Burden - Hepatic fibrosis stage
Over the past 2 weeks, histological or non-invasive staging of liver fibrosis severity

definitions.definition: The stage of hepatic fibrosis classified on a standardized scale from zero indicating no fibrosis to four indicating cirrhosis, determined by liver biopsy histology or validated non-invasive methods such as transient elastography or serum biomarker panels. Fibrosis staging is essential for prognostication and treatment decisions in chronic liver diseases including non-alcoholic steatohepatitis and viral hepatitis. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Somatic Symptom Burden - Endoscopy finding classification
During the past 4 weeks, standardized classification of findings during gastrointestinal endoscopy

definitions.definition: The categorical classification of mucosal and structural findings identified during upper or lower gastrointestinal endoscopy, recorded using standardized reporting terminology. Findings are classified by type including erosions, ulcers, polyps, strictures, and masses, and by location within the gastrointestinal tract. Endoscopic classification enables systematic documentation and facilitates comparison across follow-up examinations. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Somatic Symptom Burden - Helicobacter pylori infection status
In the past 7 days, result of diagnostic testing for Helicobacter pylori infection

definitions.definition: The result of diagnostic testing for active Helicobacter pylori infection, determined by urea breath test, stool antigen assay, or histological examination of gastric biopsy specimens. Positive status indicates current infection and guides eradication therapy with a proton pump inhibitor and antibiotic combination. H. pylori infection is causally associated with chronic gastritis, peptic ulcer disease, and gastric adenocarcinoma. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Somatic Symptom Burden - Gastrointestinal functional impairment
Over the past 2 weeks, degree of disability in daily activities due to gastrointestinal symptoms

definitions.definition: A composite measure of the extent to which gastrointestinal symptoms impair the patient's ability to perform daily activities including work, social participation, meal preparation, and physical exercise. Functional impairment is assessed using validated patient-reported outcome instruments that capture disability across multiple life domains. The measure complements symptom severity scores by quantifying the real-world impact of gastrointestinal disease on daily functioning. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: COAS Somatic Symptom Burden - Inflammatory bowel disease activity index
During the past 4 weeks, composite score quantifying disease activity in inflammatory bowel disease

definitions.definition: A validated composite score incorporating symptoms, laboratory markers, and endoscopic findings to quantify the current level of disease activity in Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis. Activity indices guide treatment escalation, de-escalation, and surgical decision-making. Clinical remission is typically defined as a score below a validated threshold. Serial assessment enables objective monitoring of treatment response. Based on the Clinical Outcome Assessment Scale (COAS).

designations.designation: Gastrointestinal medication usage
Current medications prescribed for gastrointestinal conditions

definitions.definition: A comprehensive record of all pharmacological agents prescribed for the patient's gastrointestinal condition, including proton pump inhibitors, antispasmodics, prokinetics, aminosalicylates, immunomodulators, and biologic agents. Medication name, dose, frequency, route, and duration of therapy are documented. The record enables assessment of treatment adherence and identification of drug interactions. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Celiac disease antibody titer
Serum tissue transglutaminase IgA antibody level for celiac screening

definitions.definition: The serum concentration of immunoglobulin A antibodies against tissue transglutaminase, measured in units per milliliter, used as the primary serological screening test for celiac disease. Elevated titers above the laboratory-specific threshold indicate a high probability of celiac disease and warrant confirmatory small bowel biopsy. Serial monitoring assesses adherence to a gluten-free diet. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Nutritional status assessment score
Standardized evaluation of the patient's nutritional adequacy

definitions.definition: A composite nutritional assessment score derived from anthropometric measurements, biochemical markers including serum albumin and prealbumin, dietary intake records, and clinical examination findings. The assessment identifies patients at risk of malnutrition due to gastrointestinal malabsorption, chronic inflammation, or inadequate oral intake. Nutritional screening is recommended for all patients with chronic GI disease. A field of the Patient Health Inventory (PHI).

designations.designation: Age at gastrointestinal evaluation
In the past 7 days, patient age at the time of the gastrointestinal health assessment

definitions.definition: The chronological age of the patient at the date of the gastrointestinal health evaluation. Age is a critical determinant of disease prevalence and presentation in gastroenterology. Colorectal cancer screening guidelines are age-stratified, and the prevalence of conditions such as diverticulosis and Barrett esophagus increases with advancing age. Age-appropriate reference ranges are applied to laboratory and endoscopic findings, assessed in the past 7 days.

designations.designation: Gastrointestinal comorbidity count
Over the past 2 weeks, number of concurrent conditions affecting the gastrointestinal system

definitions.definition: The total number of active comorbid conditions with established effects on gastrointestinal health, including diabetes mellitus, chronic kidney disease, thyroid disorders, and connective tissue diseases. The comorbidity count provides context for interpreting gastrointestinal symptoms and guides treatment selection by identifying potential drug interactions and contraindications. Higher comorbidity burden is associated with poorer outcomes, assessed over the past 2 weeks.

designations.designation: Gastrointestinal quality of life
Impact of gastrointestinal conditions on overall quality of life

definitions.definition: A patient-reported measure of the effect of gastrointestinal symptoms and disease on overall quality of life, encompassing physical comfort, emotional well-being, social functioning, and dietary freedom. Quality of life assessment captures the subjective burden of gastrointestinal disease that may not be reflected in objective clinical measures such as laboratory values or endoscopic scores.

designations.designation: Gastrointestinal treatment response
Clinical response to prescribed gastrointestinal therapy

definitions.definition: The clinician's overall assessment of the patient's clinical response to the prescribed gastrointestinal treatment regimen, integrating changes in symptom severity, endoscopic findings, laboratory markers, and functional status from baseline to follow-up. Treatment response is classified as complete remission, partial response, stable disease, or progressive disease to guide therapy decisions.

designations.designation: Fecal calprotectin concentration
Stool calprotectin level as a non-invasive marker of intestinal inflammation

definitions.definition: The concentration of calprotectin protein in a stool sample, measured in micrograms per gram by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay. Fecal calprotectin is a neutrophil-derived protein that serves as a sensitive and specific non-invasive biomarker of intestinal inflammation. Elevated levels above 250 micrograms per gram strongly suggest active inflammatory bowel disease and distinguish organic from functional gastrointestinal disorders.
